step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("treatment options for patients diagnosed with myelofibrosis who have JAK2 mutation")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

(Your final response must contain exactly two sentences. You must conclude with \boxed{D})
The patient exhibits classic features of primary myelofibrosis, including JAK2 V617F mutation, prominent splenomegaly (>6 cm below costal margin), constitutional symptoms (weight loss, fever, night sweats), and bone marrow fibrosis—all consistent with the revised WHO diagnostic criteria. Ruxolitinib is specifically indicated for symptomatic relief and splenomegy reduction in intermediate-to-high-risk myelofibrosis, which aligns perfectly with the current stage of this patient’s disease.
